title: Automatically hide or filter done/completed tasks vs. archive
excerpt: Pain point: when using my task board, it remains cluttered with completed tasks where I would like to see only non-completed tasks. Essentially, I would like items marked with the “done” status (or a check mark) to disap…

posts.author: rkfrcoed (Russ)
posts.cookedHtml:
Pain point: when using my task board, it remains cluttered with completed tasks where I would like to see only non-completed tasks. Essentially, I would like items marked with the “done” status (or a check mark) to disappear, but still be available under the group they were in if I need to refer to them later or still need them included in calculations.

What I have tried:

  • The archive option: not helpful because the task is then removed from the group it was in and I have to search through lots of unrelated tasks to find it again, if I need to refer to if/when it was completed later. This feature would be meaningful if it was archived within the board/group. That way, you could click an option to view archived tasks and they would show within the groups they were archived in and not as a general list. Also, archiving removes them from calculations.
  • Filtering: does allow to filter out items by a status. However, it does not automatically refresh the board when you click it like archiving does, so they remain visible. So, I have to refresh the window every time I want to clear those tasks. Maybe clicking on a checkbox or the “done” category should immediately filter out the task (could be set as an option or automation). Filtering is preferable to archiving because archived tasks are removed from the group. If they are filtered out, they are not included in columns that sum, but you can turn the filter off and then they are included.

posts.author: PolishedGeek (Polished Geek | Platinum Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @rkfrcoed - What kind of plan are you on (Pro, Enterprise, etc.)?

How often do you need to refer back to the completed items? Is it a frequent occurrence or just every now and then?

posts.author: jbir1 (Jeri Birkes)
posts.cookedHtml: We desperately want to move from Asana to Monday.com but 1 of 2 holdbacks is that we can’'t ‘see’ completed tasks easily, just like this post. In Monday.com, we do not want to delete or archive deleted tasks (there are notes in discussions we need). I thought about creating a subgroup within a group for “done” but that does not minimize the size of a very long list. We just dont want to see them. I thought about creating a filter “when a task is done hide it” so that we can go back and forth between two views (all, incomplete). Yes, project managers do need to easily see completed tasks and especially with 50 projects so no sense in asking why or how often–all the time. I tried to create a community question but I get an error when tagging. Can anyone tell me a filter that actually works so that we can toggle back and forth between WIP and done? Thanks!!

posts.author: pquadri (Paulo)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi all - was this ever solved? We desperately need this functionality as well. I moved from Asana to Monday and this is such a critical tool to have. Same as the ability of having unlimited subitems / tasks that can be completed independently of their parent items. THanks!

posts.author: sarahr (Sarah R)
posts.cookedHtml: The way that I address this is on my board templates I have multiple views set up with different filters. I always have a “main” view with all of my tasks, an “open task” view filters to only show me open items, and a “completed” view with only completed items. The only frustration I have with this is that I can’t filter out completed sub tasks until the parent task is completed.
